key_insight: Substituting 50% of Ni with Mn in Cu2NiXY4 kesterite semiconductors preserves the tetragonal structure while consistently narrowing the bandgap across the Sn/Ge/Si and S/Se family.
engineering_relevance: Mn doping offers a practical route to tune Cu2NiXY4 bandgaps for optoelectronic devices that need composition-controlled absorption and electronic properties.
method: DFT first-principles calculations on Cu2NiXY4 (X=Sn, Ge, Si; Y=S, Se)
mBJ+U used to improve bandgap accuracy
Single-site Ni→Mn substitution modeled at 50% due to kesterite cell constraints
evidence_signal: Across the studied compounds, the bandgap decreased from 1.028–3.397 eV to 1.007–3.333 eV, with Cu2NiSnS4 dropping from 1.59 eV to 1.49 eV.
watch_item: The reported Mn concentration is fixed at 50% in the unit cell, so lower-doping behavior and supercell effects remain unresolved.
paper_title: First-principles Study of Structural and Electronic Properties of Mn-doped Cu2NiXY4 (X=Sn, Ge, Si; Y=S, Se) Chalcogenide Semiconductors
